title: WRATH OF THE BREEDE
copyVersions: Mud-caked cellars, uprooted trellises, vast expanses of vineyard reduced to swampland. The historic Robertson Wine Valley rebuilds after historic floods.
A legacy built by generations of farmers, threatened by historic floods in a matter of hours. What can be saved from the wreckage?

It tore through the valley with a force locals have never seen before. More than a million bottles of wine destroyed - livelihoods and legacies threatened. But the community's spirit is harder to wash away.

title: UNCOOPERATIVE COOPERATIVE
legalNote: No mention of Anweskus anywhere (even the filenames, please)
copyVersions: They packed up their lives and moved to a remote farm in search of self-governance. But this great trek is ending, for some, in tears.
Communal living on the remote West Coast. For some, a dream come true. But now, neighbours are turning on each other in a bitter fight for survival.
They were promised an Afrikaner utopia - freedom, ownership and work. Families put everything on the line - but now they are trapped in limbo.
It began as a dream of land, work and independence. Families invested everything. But was this remote cooperative ever even legal?
They were promised an Afrikaner utopia. But some members say they now face losing their life savings as neighbours turn against each other in a fight to stay on the land.
A promised rural utopia now cracking under the strain of infighting and betrayal. Then, [second line here]

title: SKATE A NATION
copyVersions: A skateboard, a stranger, and a journey that went viral - as crowds gather, communities rally and South Africans show up for something good.
An American skater set out from Uganda to cross Africa for charity. When his Ugandan teammates were denied SA visas, he pushed on alone. Then South Africa showed up.
What does it look like when a community decides to show up? A US skateboarder witnessed the spirit of South Africa as he pushed across the continent for charity.

title: ZAMA HUNTER
copyVersions: Investigative journalist Graham Coetzer goes undercover into the shadowy world of illegal gold mining, and uncovers a criminal empire.
From desperate miners risking their lives in flooded shafts, to back-office operations - investigative journalist Graham Coetzer takes us inside the world of zama zamas.
Shadowy operations where billions in tax fraud fuel a modern gold rush - we speak to author and journalist Graham Coetzer about zama zamas.

title: LONELINESS CLUB
copyVersions: Every morning at this unassuming house, a familiar routine unfolds; music, games, conversation and care - a lifeline in a growing epidemic of loneliness.
It looks like a daycare centre, but this one's a little different. This is where pensioners meet as they seek an antidote to loneliness.
Loneliness is linked to depression, dementia and early death. But in one Cape Town suburb, a small community is fighting back - with tea, dancing, and human connection.

Some are widowed. Some are losing their memories. Others simply don't want to spend another day alone. In an age of isolation, one centre is a refuge for elderly people navigating the difficulties of growing old alone.

title: PASSION IN ACTION
copyVersions: In a corner of KwaZulu-Natal, a unique partnership is thriving. What began with seven rescued elephants has become a dream to benefit both wildlife and people.
In rural KZN, a community is building a shared future for wildlife, land and people. Can land reform and conservation work hand in hand?
Fifteen years after one man saved a doomed herd of elephants, his legacy has grown beyond conservation. This is a story of passion turned into action.

title: CONTRACT FOR VIOLENCE? / SECURITY CHECK
legalNote: No mention of Ensure anywhere (even the filenames, please)
copyVersions: Brutally beaten, driven away... Months later, he's still missing. An isolated incident, or a pattern of violence in plain sight?
A public security contract, reports of beatings, and a disappearance. A case of rogue officers, or a campaign of violence?
Reports of beatings - then, a brutal assault caught on camera. But no one has been charged. Is a security company targeting the city's most vulnerable?
An alleged pattern of violence emerges on Durban's streets. Activists say warnings were ignored. Are authorities turning a blind eye?
Reports of brutality... then an assault caught on camera. Is a pattern of violence emerging on Durban's streets? Then, [second line here]
Beatings and a disappearance... Has this city signed a contract for public safety or violence? And later, [second line here]

title: VENEZUELA: A NATION REAWAKENS
copyVersions: Its authoritarian president is gone, but Venezuela's future remains unwritten. A fragile new era of hope and uncertainty emerges.
A South African teacher living in Caracas witnessed a nation in collapse... Now, through his eyes, we see Venezuela standing at a crossroads.
It's a country historically plagued by poverty, inflation and authoritarian government. Now, its future is in flux. A new Venezuela is being built - but for whom?
Venezuela: a country slowly emerging from a past defined by crisis. We witness a future unfolding: one filled with hope, uncertainty and a scramble for oil and power.
As public life revives in Venezuela, we witness an uncertain future unfolding through the eyes of a South African in Caracas. And, [second line here]
[First line here] And then, months after the dramatic capture of its president, Venezuela's future is in flux. Who will the new era serve?

title: SHERIFF SHAMBLES
copyVersions: A deep dive into the world of sheriffs where allegations of corruption have surfaced. But the Minister of Justice says there's nothing to see here.
They're the enforcement arm of the courts, and it can be a lucrative position. But insiders say all is not well in the world of the sheriffs.
A robust interview with the Minister of Justice as insiders claim that all is not well at the South African Board for Sheriffs. Is the fund meant to protect the public being abused?
Allegations against a vital arm of the justice system... The minister insists they hold no water. Who holds the truth?

title: THE LAST RIGHT
copyVersions: Two lives, one question: when suffering becomes unbearable, who gets to decide how it ends?
It's a legal debate that's lingered for decades. At its heart, deeply personal stories. Now, a new legal challenge tests whether the law violates constitutional rights.
He wants the right to decide how it ends. On the other side, once desperate to die, she sees life as a gift. Two stories, one question: should we all have the right to end our own lives?
When dignity, suffering and choice collide... Two lives at the heart of the battle over assisted dying.
The freedom to die - or choosing life at all costs? The deeply personal stories driving our assisted dying debate.

title: SCOOTER SQUABBLE
copyVersions: A homegrown startup eases traffic and cuts transport cost. So, why does the municipality want it off the streets?
A town stuck at a crossroads: traditional traffic management, or electric mobility? Innovation meets bureaucracy.
It's a test case in a country plagued by traffic and rising fuel prices. So, why is this transport solution facing shutdown?
Innovative solution or unlicenced nuisance? They're popular and eco-friendly. But these electric scooters are causing controversy in Stellenbosch.

title: HOT SOUP / FEEDING ON FEEDING SCHEMES
copyVersions: Government spends hundreds of millions to feed the hungry. But where is the money really going?
Inflated prices, murky suppliers, and the most vulnerable going hungry. We go inside a multimillion-rand soup kitchen programme.
Dilapidated facilities, uncollected waste, rodent infestations... A programme to fight hunger is under scrutiny - and the numbers just don't add up.

title: DIGNITY DENIED
copyVersions: Millions meant for maintenance unspent, while retirement villages fall apart and elderly residents say the municipality has abandoned them.
Broken homes, sewage leaks, and no security. This is how our most vulnerable are living. With government-run housing in decline, we ask: where is the city?
Four retirement villages in decline, while elderly residents live in discomfort and fear. And behind the decay: millions budgeted for repairs, unspent.
Why have millions of rands been returned to Treasury while elderly residents live in discomfort and fear? And then, [second line here]

title: ESKOM GHOST VILLAGE
copyVersions: Nearly a billion rand spent, but not a single resident in sight... We go inside a taxpayer-funded ghost town.
It was built for workers but never used. In a province battling unemployment, it stands as a monument to waste and vanished accountability.
From a distance, nothing seems amiss. But look closer... Inflated tenders, ballooning costs - but a billion-rand development stands empty.

title: FOOTBALL FAIL
copyVersions: Once the birthplace of champions, now it's a school in crisis. Why are parents and teachers blowing the whistle?
Mass retrenchments, delayed salaries, soaring fees... And a controversial new boss at the centre of it all. How did an elite academy end up in survival mode?
Promises of excellence, but a reality of chaos. A legacy built over decades allegedly unravelled in weeks.
It was meant to transform young lives. Now, whistleblowers speak out about conditions, costs and a collapsing system. Has a dream turned into a warning?
How did an elite academy end up in survival mode? And later, [second line here]
[First line here] Then, what went wrong behind the doors of a school of soccer excellence?

title: GLENHARVIE DOWN / UNDER SIEGE
copyVersions: As mines close, a picturesque West Rand town sinks into chaos. One paramedic refuses to give up. But is he fighting a losing battle?
It looks and sounds like a war zone, but this is what happens when mines close and illegal miners move in. And residents say they've been left to fend for themselves.
It's a once-thriving town on Gauteng's West Rand. Now, armed gangs of zama zamas fight a turf war in its streets. We go inside a town where violence fills the vacuum left by the mines.
As mines close, violence fills the vacuum: inside a West Rand town where residents say they're fighting a lose battle.
Running gun battles in broad daylight, executions, vandalism  and arson. This is the tale of a town under siege. Then, [second line here]

title: WHAT A STINK / STINK IN PARADISE
copyVersions: A farming paradise turned toxic... Failing health, businesses at risk, a community under siege - while appeals to authorities fall on deaf ears.
It looks like unspoiled, peaceful farmland - until the wind changes. Alleged toxic emissions, contaminated roads, and serious health concerns. Is this company putting profit before people?
An international company with a name for sustainability. But closer to home, the reality stinks. And enforcement is apparently nowhere to be seen.
Inside, a multimillion-rand operation. Outside, reeking air, alleged polluted water, and unanswered questions. What's the human cost of turning waste into profit?
Raising a stink! Is a company putting profit before people? And, [second line here]

title: MASTER OF RIVERS
copyVersions: From childhood passion to a record-breaking world champion. We bring you the story of a lifetime on the water and meet the man behind the legacy.
It's one of the world's toughest races; a test of strength, skill, and focus. And one man is the undisputed champion of the river.
Three gruelling days on water and dirt. The Dusi Canoe Marathon tests everything, and one man holds an extraordinary place in its history.

title: MISSING IN KZN / WITHOUT A TRACE
copyVersions: A series of chilling crimes in KZN ignites fear and horror. Are these isolated tragedies, or signs of something more sinister?
A missing tourist, a handful of allegedly unexplored leads, and fears of something far more sinister.
She went for a walk and never returned... In this quiet, picturesque bushveld, something doesn't add up. We investigate allegations of police inaction, and even more chilling theories.
In a remote rural community, a series of chilling crimes has ignited fears of a sinister trade.

title: RIVERS FOR SALE / WATER GRAB
copyVersions: A thirsty city, a hidden trade and water that's worth more than gold. We ask: who's controlling the water?
In Gqeberha, a brazen underground trade has taken hold; illegal boreholes, municipal supplies siphoned, polluted rivers pumped. In a city running dry, who's controlling the water?
In a thirsty city, water is more than survival - it's business. Polluted rivers, stolen supply, a system out of control. As enforcement fails, an alleged illegal trade flourishes.
In a city running dry, water tankers are welcomed saviours. But a shadow industry is cashing in. Then, [second line here]

title: GUN SHOP HEIST
copyVersions: Five years ago, we exposed a dodgy solar deal; panels never arrived and promises evaporated in the sun. Now, allegations emerge again in a game of contracts and cartridges.

Big promises followed by missing payments... Firearms dealers say their businesses were quietly taken over. We bring you this story of shadow deals, stolen directorships and weapons in the wrong hands.
Years ago, we confronted him over a solar scandal. This time, it's not panels, but pistols. Shops allegedly hijacked, guns missing. A familiar suspected conman enters the firearm trade.
A gun shop, alleged missing payments, and a man we've met before... Then, [second line here]

title: CRAYFISH CRACKDOWN or CRAYFISH & CRIME
copyVersions: It's a war fought on our coastlines and waterways: poachers, hunting protected species out of season. We meet the unit battling crime for conservation.
It's a trade fuelled by poverty, demand, and organised crime. Now, a task force is moving in. We take you Inside the war for our coastline.
Once community vigilantes, now they work with law enforcement: stakeouts, high-risk busts, and roadside seizures. We join the patrol on waterways under siege.
For some, it's quick cash. For others, it's a sign of a collapsing ecosystem. This is a story of intrigue, risk and the race to protect threatened species.
This community-driven anti-poaching unit is determined to reclaim our marine wildlife. And, [second line here].

title: WHERE'S THE MONEY?
copyVersions: When business rescue practitioners and liquidators become targets, what dark secrets are being protected? And what's the cost of following the money?
He was gunned down in his own office. Whose secrets was he about to expose? We go inside the shadowy world of financial crime.
Two men posing as clients. Minutes later, a man is dead... As assassinations linked to organised crime are on the rise, we investigate the dirty money behind the violence.
A murder in Joburg's leafy suburbs... Our investigation leads down a dark path as we peer into the murky world of financial crime.
A dark world of financial crime and shadowy figures - we follow the money.

title: POVERTY FOR PROFIT
copyVersions: Millions allocated to upgrade an informal settlement. On paper, a story of development and upliftment. But what's the reality on the ground?
Murders, arrests and allegations of bribery and intimidation. We tell this story of crime, corruption and claims of poverty for profit.
A man behind bars faces charges of murder. But he claims he's a whistleblower exposing corruption. We test the claims as tensions escalate.
A letter, a murder and allegations of corruption. Who is guilty? And where did the money go?

title: THE CUNNING CON
copyVersions: A conman we exposed nearly a decade ago is still targeting farmers. And he's doing it from inside a prison cell!
Fake identities, fake proof of payment, livestock disappearing overnight... We follow the trail of a stolen flock and demand answers.
Farmers conned out of their livestock by a man who sounds like one of them. But the voice on the phone belongs to a convicted fraudster.
Locked up, but not shut down... A master conman is robbing farmers and every move is orchestrated from behind bars.
Farmers are being duped and handing over their livestock to a jailed master conman!

title: LURED TO WAR / WAR GAMES
copyVersions: Seven months on the frontline of war. Now, they return home. But who sent them to near-certain death?
Persuaded to join a war on the other side of the world... Many have returned, but not all their families are rejoicing.
It's a bittersweet homecoming with the horrors of war not easily left behind. And for one family, there's no relief.
After seven months on the Russo-Ukraine frontline. Several South Africans return home. But their families aren't rejoicing.

title: BAY OF BROKEN DREAMS
copyVersions: A December tender sparks outrage. In a city plagued by allegations of tender corruption and mismanagement, will animals be the next to suffer?
Thousands of street lights out, rubbish swirling in the main street... Nelson Mandela Bay is a metro in collapse. Now, will animals be the next to suffer?
Streets left in the dark and sewage untreated. Files missing, billions untraceable... Have truth and accountability vanished too?
Sewage untreated, rubbish swirling in the streets. Residents left outraged and desperate. In Nelson Mandela Bay, the lights are going out.
A metro in decline - what's really going on in Nelson Mandela Bay?

title: FOREIGN FIGHTER FILE
copyVersions: South Africans who fought alongside Russian troops are returning home. Now, they face a critical question: what happens to those who violated our laws governing military service?
At 19, she walked into an embassy in Pretoria. Days later, she was on her way to a warzone. Today, she's firsthand proof that Ukraine has sent our citizens to the frontlines.
South Africans fighting a foreign war; some on opposite sides of the same conflict. Now, they return home. But will they face legal repercussions?
She joined a conflict on the other side of the world. She feared becoming a symbol - or worse, a statistic. Now, she faces prosecution and legal limbo.
She joined the Ukrainian war effort, but fled months later. We meet another South African on the Russo-Ukraine frontline.

title: SUGARCOATED COLLAPSE / SUGAR COLLAPSE
copyVersions: For more than a century, sugar built an empire along this coast. Now, as a corporate giant faces possible liquidation, entire towns stand on the brink. What's the human cost of industrial collapse?
Along this coast, sugar is more than just a crop. But one of the country's oldest economies faces corporate collapse, and a government allegedly failing to protect local industry.
In northern KwaZulu-Natal, entire towns were built around one crop - generations shaped by sugar. When the mill stops turning, what happens to the people left behind?
One of our oldest agricultural giants is now expected to fall. For these families, the future has never looked so uncertain. When an economy dissolves, who pays the price?

title: THE SCIENCE OF LAUGHTER
copyVersions: It's work that demands a keen sense of timing. But these entertainers perform for more than applause... Meet the heroes sharing the healing power of laughter.
Their job is simple: to make children laugh. But here, it means so much more. It's about dignity, healing, and reclaiming childhood in the face of illness.
In our most overstretched hospitals, children and their families face illness and grief. But support comes from an unlikely source. We meet the heroes prescribing a quiet, unlikely brand of healing.

title: RENT'S DUE IN JOBURG
copyVersions: Millions in rent allegedly flowing to one business empire - even as government vows to reduce leases, and state-owned buildings stand empty.
R34 million a month in rent - from taxpayers' pockets. Nearly a year later, where is the promised investigation? And why do government-owned buildings stand empty?
A government precinct that never materialised. While leases worth hundreds of millions have been approved,  we ask: what's happening to the buildings now standing empty?
Hundreds of buildings stand empty... So, why is government spending millions in leases? And who benefits?

title: PRIDE, POLLUTION AND PREJUDICE
copyVersions: Is a historic community in the American South being wiped off the map, house by house? Residents claim a South African petrochemical giant is at the centre of it all.
A community built by descendants of enslaved people. Now, its last families say they're being squeezed out by pollution and corporate muscle.
It's a flagship South African company, celebrated for transformation and empowerment. But is it driving a black community on the other side of the world to the brink?
What does a community in the industrial heart of Louisiana have to do with South Africa? A story of vanishing heritage, a petrochemical giant, and a community's fight for survival.

title: GOODBYE SANDTON
copyVersions: Million-rand properties in prime locations. But behind high walls, syndicates move in. We follow the trail of illegal connections, homes hijacked, and stolen profits.
Dozens of properties, hundreds of illegal residents. And at the centre: well-organised criminal operations. We look at the trail of destruction left by syndicates thriving in Joburg's luxury suburbs.
Homes hijacked. Utilities stolen. Millions lost. A property syndicate profits while the city and its residents pay the price.

title: CRYPTO CON
copyVersions: From crypto kings to global fugitives... It's a journey of tax havens, a Swiss vault, and another rug pull. How did two whizz kids turn a heist into a lifestyle?
It was the cyber heist of the century; billions in Bitcoin vanished overnight. But where are the self-styled tech geniuses who pulled it off?
Luxury cars, expensive restaurants, multimillion-dollar homes... Crypto high-flyers make investors an offer they can't refuse. A story of living it up, to losing it all.

They were barely more than kids when they pulled off Africa's biggest crypto scam. Now, they face new accusations of theft and fraud. How did the golden-boys-turned-fugitives end up rich, free and living large?
Crypto golden boys on the run... Find out how they skipped the country, what they spent their loot on, and how they got away with it all.
They offered friendship with a golden opportunity. Then, they vanished with billions in Bitcoin.

title: N2 HELL RUN
copyVersions: It's been called the Hell Run... Day or night, no time is safe. Will a controversial plan to build a wall stop the scourge?
Cameras, cages, cops. So, why is this highway still one of the most dangerous in the country? And is a multimillion-rand wall the answer?
They appear from nowhere and vanish in seconds... Their target: commuters. A national highway is under attack. We ask: are authorities losing ground?

title: MURDER IN PARADISE
copyVersions: For over two decades, he allegedly wreaked havoc. Now, a murder brings a dark history to light.
A seaside town popular with holidaymakers... Its peace shattered by a brutal murder. But are there more sinister secrets waiting to be revealed?
A seasoned former police detective is found dead. Was this an isolated case, or the finale to decades of alleged abuse? What secrets lie beneath this small-town murder?

title: LIVESTOCK LOCKDOWN
copyVersions: It's one of the world's most contagious livestock diseases with devastating consequences for food security and livelihoods. So, how did delay become the deadliest part of the outbreak?
A devastating livestock disease sweeps the country. Markets collapse and families are pushed to the brink, while solutions drown in red tape.
Auctions postponed, sales halted: livestock farmers are in lockdown. No relief fund, no timeline and no vaccine. As infection spreads, the human cost is escalating.
From mass animal burials to bankrupt farms... We expose the system that stalled while a foot and mouth epidemic exploded.
How has bureaucracy - not biology - let foot and mouth run wild?

title: GOING TO WAR
copyVersions: They're leaving everything they know behind to fight a war on foreign soil. We investigate how underground recruiters lure Africans into an active war zone.

A years-long conflict on the other side of the world has cost hundreds of thousands of lives. Now, a world power is seeking soldiers from much closer to home. Who are the Africans risking everything on the Russian frontlines?
Sent to the frontline with minimal training to become casualties of another country's conflict. Are vulnerable Africans being sent to a certain death?
Recruiters promise fast cash and a new life, and novices from Africa sign contracts they can't escape.
Why are Zimbabweans in South Africa signing up to fight in a foreign war which has already cost hundreds of thousands of lives?

title: LIMPOPO FLOODS
copyVersions: Homes swept away, roads washed out and critical infrastructure on the brink. In Limpopo, floods rewrite the landscape and the lives of those trapped in their path.
Flood risk alert level: maximum. As the number of fatalities climbs and communities remain isolated, we visit a province under water.
A relentless downpour leaves roads wrecked and communities devastated. As authorities struggle to keep up with the rising waters, we ask: Is Limpopo's disaster a climate change warning?

title: FOOTBALL FACTORY
copyVersions: A club that made legends, now reduced to rubble. How did South Africa's football factory fall into ruin? The rise, the fall, and the legacy of a lost icon.
Fields that once produced champions now lie overgrown. From political meddling to mismanagement, we uncover the hidden story behind a football factory's collapse.
It was once home to players who changed the nation's game. Now, it's legacy is lost due to a system that let this football gem slip away.

title: FLIGHT TO BANJUL
copyVersions: Fired on a string of charges... A decade later, he resurfaced with a new title, a new job and, allegedly, the same old habits. We follow the rise, fall and rise again of one controversial politician.

A delegation to West Africa, a string of cancellations worth nearly R2 million, and a province collapsing under service delivery failures and poor leadership. How did a disgraced public official slip back into power - and the public purse?
A man once dismissed for misconduct returns to public office. Now, a struggling province faces a scandal over millions allegedly wasted. And our investigation takes an unexpected turn.

title: DEATH IN THE INNER CITY
copyVersions: He wanted to fight crime in the inner city. Instead, he became a victim. How did one man's mission to restore Joburg put him in the firing line?
A plan to combat urban decay ends in tragedy. It's a story about risk, hidden violence, and the long arm of organised crime.
Gunshots in broad daylight, a sudden death in Joburg's inner city. How did a media favourite fall victim to organised crime?

title: BOPHUTHATSWANA
copyVersions: Stadiums abandoned. Factories overgrown. In the ruins of a former homeland, a controversial question rises: was life better under Lucas Mangope?

Thirty years into democracy, nostalgia meets outrage. Some claim order and opportunity while others recall violence and repression under Lucas Mangope. Is current service delivery failure fuelling myths of a golden age?
Crumbling factories and collapsing stadiums: monuments to what once was. But was Bophuthatswana ever the success some remember? What drives nostalgia for a complicated past?

title: BRIBE FOR A PILLOW
copyVersions: He promised lucrative accommodation deal, showed invoices, made guarantees... Then left property owners in the red.
An alleged property scam with a unique South African flavour. This is a case of flip flops, bribes and Eskom.

title: UNDER THE COUNTER: ILLEGAL CIGARETTES
copyVersions: They come in their hundreds, day in and day out. Smugglers are hauling illegal cigarettes across the border, and it's a war zone on the ground.
An illegal trade is costing the country billions. From border smugglers to shop counters, we trace the trade in illicit cigarettes.
From spaza shop shelves to brazen cross-border hauls. It's more than a cheap smoke. It's a multi-billion rand criminal economy bleeding the country dry.

title: DEATH BEHIND BARS
copyVersions: An overcrowded prison and an awaiting-trial inmate beaten and naked. His wounds tell the story of a horrific death. We investigate.
One inmate versus many warders... An alleged gangster dies in a prison fight and a mother insists her son has been murdered. What really happened to Quintin Fortuin?
An incident in prison involving drugs, dogs and broken cameras... Who is to blame for the death of an inmate behind bars?
An alleged gangster dies awaiting trial and the official story raises as many questions as answers. What really happened in this Cape Town prison?

title: SPINNING WITH SAM SAM
copyVersions: He's the face of a viral phenomenon that started right here at home. But it's not just cars he's spinning; it's culture, identity and possibility.
From Mbombela's streets to the world stage... We meet a global superstar on the rise and taste the thrill of a viral motorsport phenomenon.
It's a global street culture phenomenon with uniquely local roots, and one young man is spinning his way into history.
